message:
chore(beads): close yj7h, m5nf, gcmq (upstream-aligned)

Closed via direct JSONL edit (per memory feedback_br_sync_drift).
All three fixes shipped in b5a3263.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id": "frankenredis-4upy", "title": "fr-runtime: CLIENT LIST output diverges from upstream Redis 7.2 (missing argv-mem/multi-mem/rbs/rbp/redir, extra watch, flags position)", "description": "fr-runtime's CLIENT LIST output diverges from upstream Redis 7.2 in several ways: (1) MISSING fields: argv-mem (input argv memory), multi-mem (queued MULTI memory), rbs (read-buffer size), rbp (read-buffer position), redir (CLIENT TRACKING REDIRECT target id, default -1). (2) EXTRA field: watch=N (count of WATCHed keys) — Redis 7.2 dropped this field. (3) FIELD ORDER: fr emits flags=N at the very end; Redis 7.2 emits flags right after idle, before db. (4) lib-name / lib-ver order: Redis emits these AFTER resp; fr emits before. (5) fd is hardcoded to 0 vs Redis's actual epoll fd — minor but a real divergence; tools that grep on fd=N may misbehave.

Acceptance: a fresh fr-server with one connected client emits CLIENT LIST in a single line whose tokens (and order) match upstream Redis 7.2's CLIENT LIST output for an equivalent client, modulo the fd field (fd=0 stays since fr doesn't track real fds for clients) and the per-instance values (id, addr port, qbuf, qbuf-free, tot-mem). Tools like redis_exporter that parse the line on field names (regex) will match. The conformance harness's CLIENT LIST live-oracle test gains a side-by-side text diff that asserts identical token ordering.

Reference: ran 'CLIENT LIST' against fr (port 16500) and vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16501) with the same shape of single connected redis-cli client. fr line: 'id=2 addr=... laddr=... fd=0 name= age=0 idle=0 db=0 sub=0 psub=0 ssub=0 multi=-1 watch=0 qbuf=0 qbuf-free=0 obl=0 oll=0 omem=0 tot-mem=0 events=r cmd=client|list user=default lib-name= lib-ver= resp=2 flags=N'. Redis line: 'id=3 addr=... laddr=... fd=7 name= age=0 idle=0 flags=N db=0 sub=0 psub=0 ssub=0 multi=-1 qbuf=26 qbuf-free=20448 argv-mem=10 multi-mem=0 rbs=16384 rbp=16384 obl=0 oll=0 omem=0 tot-mem=37786 events=r cmd=client|list user=default redir=-1 resp=2 lib-name= lib-ver='.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "assignee": "cc_1", "created_at": "2026-04-29T22:08:17.262396364Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T02:30:29.370108Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "closed_at": "2026-04-30T02:30:29.370108Z", "close_reason": "Both fr-runtime's client_info_line_for_session and fr-command's client_info_line now emit CLIENT LIST/INFO with upstream Redis 7.2 field order: id addr laddr fd name age idle flags db sub psub ssub multi qbuf qbuf-free argv-mem multi-mem rbs rbp obl oll omem tot-mem events cmd user redir resp lib-name lib-ver. Dropped watch=N (removed in 7.2), added redir + buffer-accounting fields (always 0 since fr doesn't track per-client buffers). Pinned by client_list_field_order_matches_upstream_redis_72 in fr-runtime. Shipped in 057c446."}
+{"id":"frankenredis-4upy","title":"fr-runtime: CLIENT LIST output diverges from upstream Redis 7.2 (missing argv-mem/multi-mem/rbs/rbp/redir, extra watch, flags position)","description":"fr-runtime's CLIENT LIST output diverges from upstream Redis 7.2 in several ways: (1) MISSING fields: argv-mem (input argv memory), multi-mem (queued MULTI memory), rbs (read-buffer size), rbp (read-buffer position), redir (CLIENT TRACKING REDIRECT target id, default -1). (2) EXTRA field: watch=N (count of WATCHed keys) — Redis 7.2 dropped this field. (3) FIELD ORDER: fr emits flags=N at the very end; Redis 7.2 emits flags right after idle, before db. (4) lib-name / lib-ver order: Redis emits these AFTER resp; fr emits before. (5) fd is hardcoded to 0 vs Redis's actual epoll fd — minor but a real divergence; tools that grep on fd=N may misbehave.

Acceptance: a fresh fr-server with one connected client emits CLIENT LIST in a single line whose tokens (and order) match upstream Redis 7.2's CLIENT LIST output for an equivalent client, modulo the fd field (fd=0 stays since fr doesn't track real fds for clients) and the per-instance values (id, addr port, qbuf, qbuf-free, tot-mem). Tools like redis_exporter that parse the line on field names (regex) will match. The conformance harness's CLIENT LIST live-oracle test gains a side-by-side text diff that asserts identical token ordering.

Reference: ran 'CLIENT LIST' against fr (port 16500) and vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16501) with the same shape of single connected redis-cli client. fr line: 'id=2 addr=... laddr=... fd=0 name= age=0 idle=0 db=0 sub=0 psub=0 ssub=0 multi=-1 watch=0 qbuf=0 qbuf-free=0 obl=0 oll=0 omem=0 tot-mem=0 events=r cmd=client|list user=default lib-name= lib-ver= resp=2 flags=N'. Redis line: 'id=3 addr=... laddr=... fd=7 name= age=0 idle=0 flags=N db=0 sub=0 psub=0 ssub=0 multi=-1 qbuf=26 qbuf-free=20448 argv-mem=10 multi-mem=0 rbs=16384 rbp=16384 obl=0 oll=0 omem=0 tot-mem=37786 events=r cmd=client|list user=default redir=-1 resp=2 lib-name= lib-ver='.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-29T22:08:17.262396364Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T02:28:49.600852964Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T02:28:49.598825193Z","close_reason":"Both fr-runtime's client_info_line_for_session and fr-command's client_info_line now emit CLIENT LIST/INFO with upstream Redis 7.2 field order: id addr laddr fd name age idle flags db sub psub ssub multi qbuf qbuf-free argv-mem multi-mem rbs rbp obl oll omem tot-mem events cmd user redir resp lib-name lib-ver. Dropped watch=N (removed in 7.2), added redir + buffer-accounting fields (always 0 since fr doesn't track per-client buffers). Pinned by client_list_field_order_matches_upstream_redis_72 in fr-runtime. Shipped in 057c446.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-4x9a", "title": "redis parity: runtime EVAL BGREWRITEAOF regression should expect NOSCRIPT", "description": "Full fr-runtime runs are still red on eval_bgrewriteaof_rewrites_aof_snapshot because command parity now rejects BGREWRITEAOF from Lua with NOSCRIPT, but the runtime regression still expects the old success reply. Update the runtime expectation to the Redis-aligned NOSCRIPT surface and verify through rch.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-21T18:50:31.754862465Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-21T18:54:47.075307402Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-21T18:54:47.072900897Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-4y2a", "title": "redis parity: ACL SETUSER should support skip-sanitize-payload", "description": "Upstream ACLSetUser in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/acl.c supports both skip-sanitize-payload and sanitize-payload, but local AuthState::set_user at crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:751 only treats sanitize-payload as a no-op and has no state for the skip variant. Add explicit sanitize mode state plus GETUSER, ACL LIST, and ACL SAVE parity for the two flags.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-21T09:11:24.199344491Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-21T09:20:38.175078835Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-21T09:20:38.172833161Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

 {"id": "frankenredis-gbv5", "title": "Add fixed empty-policy empty FUNCTION RESTORE regression", "description": "fr-store already treats an empty FUNCTION RESTORE policy as APPEND and already pins explicit APPEND behavior on empty dumps, but it does not freeze the default-policy plus empty-dump path directly. Add a focused regression proving an empty policy with an empty FUNCTION DUMP payload is a byte-stable no-op over an existing multi-library snapshot.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-23T01:33:00Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-23T01:33:00Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-23T01:33:00Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["functions", "persistence", "testing"]}
 {"id": "frankenredis-gbv6", "title": "Add metamorphic empty-policy empty FUNCTION RESTORE regression", "description": "fr-store now has a fixed regression for empty-policy FUNCTION RESTORE against the empty dump sentinel, but the adjacent metamorphic suite still skips that default-policy path. Add a generated-library property proving an empty policy with an empty FUNCTION DUMP payload is a snapshot-preserving, byte-stable no-op across randomized library sets.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-23T01:39:00Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-23T01:39:00Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-23T01:39:00Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["functions", "metamorphic", "persistence", "testing"]}
 {"id": "frankenredis-gc45", "title": "Add fuzz target for PSYNC reply parser", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-17T05:47:17.803541444Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-17T05:59:45.913298394Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-17T05:59:45.911459955Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
+{"id": "frankenredis-gcmq", "title": "fr-runtime: CLUSTER SLAVES (deprecated alias) returns 'unknown subcommand' instead of upstream's behavior", "description": "fr-runtime's classify_cluster_subcommand at crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:1522 doesn't recognize SLAVES (the deprecated alias for REPLICAS), so it falls through to ClusterSubcommand::Unknown which returns 'ERR unknown subcommand SLAVES. Try CLUSTER HELP.' Upstream Redis 7.2 still accepts SLAVES (per legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/cluster-slaves.json) and reports 'ERR This instance has cluster support disabled' (when cluster is off) or the actual replica array (when cluster is on).

Acceptance: 'CLUSTER SLAVES <node-id>' against fr returns 'ERR This instance has cluster support disabled' on a non-cluster instance, matching upstream byte-for-byte. (When cluster mode is enabled it should behave identically to CLUSTER REPLICAS.)

Reference: fr port 16700 vs vendored redis port 16701, 'CLUSTER SLAVES abc'. fr: 'ERR unknown subcommand SLAVES. Try CLUSTER HELP.'. redis: 'ERR This instance has cluster support disabled'. Caught by ad-hoc differential probe.

Fix is straightforward: add SLAVES to the cluster subcommand classifier in fr-runtime, and add a SLAVES branch in fr-command::cluster_cmd that mirrors the REPLICAS branch (since SLAVES is just the legacy alias).", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "bug", "assignee": "cc_1", "created_at": "2026-04-30T02:54:16.703448986Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T03:09:20.993170Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "closed_at": "2026-04-30T03:09:20.993170Z", "close_reason": "CLUSTER SLAVES now accepted as the upstream-deprecated alias for REPLICAS. Returns 'ERR This instance has cluster support disabled' on non-cluster instance, matching vendored redis 7.2.4 byte-for-byte. Shipped in b5a3263."}
 {"id": "frankenredis-gcq", "title": "Align live CLIENT handler with fail-closed unsupported tracking semantics", "description": "fr-runtime still accepts unsupported CLIENT TRACKING/CACHING and has drifted from fr-command/runtime intent; fix live semantics and add regressions for server-visible mismatches.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-03-24T00:35:49.487481045Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-24T00:37:43.265406838Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-24T00:37:43.264929856Z", "close_reason": "Live CLIENT TRACKING/CACHING now fail closed again", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-gdpn", "title": "Add MONITOR/MODULE/FAILOVER/SENTINEL conformance fixtures", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-01T21:06:15.613976277Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-01T21:06:21.348471118Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-01T21:06:21.348200415Z", "close_reason": "27 conformance cases added in core_module_sentinel.json covering MONITOR, MODULE (LIST/HELP/LOAD/LOADEX/UNLOAD), FAILOVER, SENTINEL (HELP/MYID/MASTERS/MASTER/REPLICAS/GET-MASTER-ADDR-BY-NAME/CKQUORUM/FAILOVER/RESET/REMOVE)", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-gess", "title": "Record initial performance baselines and check into baselines/ directory", "description": "# Initial Baselines

## What
Run the benchmark harness against both FrankenRedis and legacy Redis, record the results, and check them into baselines/.

## Why
The baselines/ directory is empty. Without baselines, there is no way to detect performance regressions or prove improvements. The README claims "profile-proven performance" which requires actual numbers.

## Implementation
1. Build frankenredis in release mode: `cargo build --release -p fr-server`
2. Start frankenredis on port 6399
3. Run fr_benchmark against it with standard workloads:
   - SET only: 100k requests, 50 clients, pipeline 1
   - GET only: 100k requests, 50 clients, pipeline 1 (pre-populate keys first)
   - Mixed 50/50 SET/GET: 100k requests, 50 clients
   - Pipeline depth 16: 100k requests, 50 clients
   - INCR: 100k requests, 50 clients
4. Stop frankenredis, start legacy redis-server on same port
5. Run identical workloads against legacy Redis
6. Record all results as JSON in baselines/:
   - baselines/frankenredis_v0.1.0_set.json
   - baselines/frankenredis_v0.1.0_get.json
   - baselines/frankenredis_v0.1.0_mixed.json
   - baselines/frankenredis_v0.1.0_pipeline16.json
   - baselines/frankenredis_v0.1.0_incr.json
   - baselines/redis_7.2_set.json (comparison anchor)
   - baselines/redis_7.2_get.json
   - etc.
7. Create scripts/record_baselines.sh that automates steps 1-6
8. Create scripts/compare_baselines.py that reads two baseline files and reports deltas

## Validation
- baselines/ contains at least 10 JSON files (5 workloads x 2 servers)
- Each JSON has: ops_sec, p50_us, p95_us, p99_us, p999_us, total_time_sec, total_requests
- FrankenRedis throughput is at least 50% of legacy Redis for basic SET/GET (single-threaded, so parity is expected)
- If FrankenRedis is slower than 50% of Redis, create a follow-up bead to investigate

## Depends On
- Benchmark harness (bxlo) — CLOSED, already implemented

## Files
- baselines/*.json (new, many files)
- scripts/record_baselines.sh (new)
- scripts/compare_baselines.py (new)", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:10:23.143539757Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T20:29:52.009288902Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T20:29:52.009108591Z", "close_reason": "Recorded initial baselines, added automation scripts, and filed frankenredis-zjii for severe perf gap", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "frankenredis-gess", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-bxlo", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:16.601521835Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
@@ -505,6 +506,7 @@
 {"id": "frankenredis-lze", "title": "Reconcile residual auth drift note with current ACL baseline", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "docs", "created_at": "2026-03-28T14:14:18.402061978Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-28T14:15:10.288622527Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-28T14:15:10.288317950Z", "close_reason": "Reconciled residual auth drift note with current ACL baseline", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-m047", "title": "test(store): add metamorphic coverage for stream dump-restore invariants", "description": "The fr-store metamorphic DUMP/RESTORE suite now covers strings, lists, sets, hashes, sorted sets, and BusyKey atomicity, but not the stream encoder/decoder path. Add property coverage that seeds bounded stream entry sequences with optional TTL, restores from self-generated DUMP payloads, asserts entry/id parity and stable re-encoding, and closes the bead with durable JSONL only.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-22T22:19:00.472210155Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-22T22:21:04.344862427Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-22T22:21:04.343025718Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-m17o", "title": "v8s5 part d: upstream-compatible DUMP/RESTORE for STREAM + MIGRATE integration test", "description": "Extend DUMP/RESTORE to cover STREAM values (RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_3 = 21 at the DUMP-payload level; depends on s5su-e for the encoder). Then exercise the full loop:

  * MIGRATE host port key dest-db timeout from frankenredis → vendored redis-server moves the key end-to-end.
  * MIGRATE in reverse moves the key back.
  * COPY from one frankenredis DB to another uses the same pipeline internally.
  * XINFO STREAM FULL on the destination matches the source after each hop.

Closes v8s5 acceptance.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:17:59.895967288Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T23:03:23.928202495Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-24T23:03:23.926174134Z", "close_reason": "live_redis_stream_dump_restore_roundtrip_matches_upstream test added: our DUMP→oracle RESTORE→XRANGE echoes 4 deterministic stream entries (single-/multi-field, binary, fixed IDs); oracle XADD→DUMP→our restore_key→xrange echoes same. Confirms STREAM_LISTPACKS_3 encoder (6zk9) + decoder (qi6z) are upstream-compatible for MIGRATE. No runtime/store changes required.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
+{"id": "frankenredis-m5nf", "title": "fr-command: LOLWUT default emits ASCII art instead of upstream's 'Redis ver. X' single-line text", "description": "fr-command's lolwut_cmd at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:6438 always generates a 12-line generated_lolwut_art() ASCII grid plus 'FrankenRedis ver. 0.1.0 -- LOLWUT v1' footer. Upstream Redis 7.2's lolwutCommand (legacy_redis_code/redis/src/lolwut.c:54) routes by REDIS_VERSION:
  - REDIS_VERSION starts with '5.' (and not '5.9') → lolwut5Command (Schotter cubes art)
  - REDIS_VERSION starts with '6.' (and not '6.9') → lolwut6Command (braille art)
  - otherwise (including 7.x) → lolwutUnstableCommand: 'Redis ver. <REDIS_VERSION>\n'

Acceptance: 'LOLWUT' (no args) against an fr-server returns a single line 'FrankenRedis ver. <FR_VERSION>\n' (or 'Redis ver. ...' if we want strict client compatibility — we already report ourselves as 'Redis' in some places like INFO server section). 'LOLWUT VERSION 5' returns the Schotter cubes art (lolwut5 port), 'LOLWUT VERSION 6' returns the braille art (lolwut6 port). For a first pass the VERSION 5/6 paths can stay XFAIL and just the no-arg path matches upstream.

Reference: fr port 16700 returns the multi-line ASCII grid; redis port 16701 returns 'Redis ver. 7.2.4'. Caught by ad-hoc differential probe. Default version dispatch in upstream is in lolwut.c::lolwutCommand.", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "bug", "assignee": "cc_1", "created_at": "2026-04-30T02:54:29.942095588Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T03:09:20.993170Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "closed_at": "2026-04-30T03:09:20.993170Z", "close_reason": "LOLWUT now emits upstream's unstableCommand body ('Redis ver. <version>\n') instead of bespoke ASCII art. Matches vendored redis 7.2.4 modulo the patch number (fr reports 7.2.0 from REDIS_COMPAT_VERSION). VERSION 5/6 args accepted but emit same body — Schotter/braille art ports left as follow-up. Shipped in b5a3263."}

 {"id": "frankenredis-nwgw", "title": "Move SLOWLOG state to Store so dispatch_argv path returns real data", "description": "# Move SLOWLOG state to Store for Lua/dispatch_argv compatibility

## Problem
SLOWLOG GET/LEN/RESET in fr-command (the dispatch_argv path) returns empty arrays/0/OK because the slow log lives in Runtime's ServerState. Lua scripts calling redis.call(\"SLOWLOG\", \"GET\") get nothing.

## Design
Move the slowlog data structure from ServerState to Store:
- pub slowlog: VecDeque<SlowlogEntry>
- pub slowlog_id_counter: u64
- pub slowlog_log_slower_than_us: i64
- pub slowlog_max_len: usize

## Implementation
1. Define SlowlogEntry in fr-store (or move from fr-runtime):
   ```rust
   pub struct SlowlogEntry {
       pub id: u64,
       pub timestamp_sec: u64,
       pub duration_us: u64,
       pub argv: Vec<Vec<u8>>,
   }
   ```
2. Add the fields to Store
3. Add Store methods: record_slowlog(), get_slowlog(), slowlog_len(), reset_slowlog()
4. Update fr-command's slowlog_cmd to accept &mut Store and call these methods
5. Update fr-runtime to call store.record_slowlog() instead of server.record_slowlog()
6. Remove duplicate SlowlogEntry from runtime (or re-export from store)

## Why Store instead of Runtime
- Store is accessible from both dispatch_argv (Lua scripts, AOF replay) and the runtime
- The slow log is essentially per-database state, making Store the natural home
- This follows the same pattern we used for CONFIG GET/SET

## Migration path
- The runtime already has record_slowlog wired into execute_frame — just redirect to store
- slowlog_log_slower_than_us and slowlog_max_len are already CONFIG SET-able in runtime — redirect to store fields

## Testing
- Conformance: Lua script calls redis.call(\"SLOWLOG\", \"GET\") and gets real entries
- Unit test: record a slow command, verify SLOWLOG LEN returns 1

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (SlowlogEntry struct, Store fields, methods)
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (slowlog_cmd accepts Store, reads real data)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (redirect slowlog recording to store)", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:20:25.774798027Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-03T14:12:03.227702446Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-03T14:12:03.227428628Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
-{"id": "frankenredis-nz2v", "title": "fr-command: GEOPOS coordinate format diverges from upstream %.17Lg (16 vs 17 digits)", "description": "GEOPOS emits coordinate doubles using Rust's f64 Display (Ryu shortest round-trip, typically 16 significant digits) where upstream Redis emits via addReplyHumanLongDouble formatted with %.17Lg (17 significant digits). Result: fr returns 0.9999999403953552 / 2.000000185646549 where Redis returns 0.99999994039535522 / 2.0000001856465488 for the same encoded geohash score. The actual lat/lng resolution is identical (same bit pattern decodes to the same point on the geoid), but the wire format differs in the trailing digit count.

Impact: low. Geo precision is dominated by the geohash bit budget (52 bits, ~0.6m at the equator), so the 17th digit is below noise. But this is a real wire-format divergence that breaks byte-equal diff tests.

Acceptance: GEOPOS / GEOSEARCH WITHCOORD format coordinate doubles with 17 significant digits in shortest-form (matches upstream %.17Lg). One reasonable Rust path: add a fr-command::format_redis_double(value: f64) -> String helper using a custom snprintf-style formatter, or use the printf crate. Conformance core_geo cases that currently XFAIL on GEOPOS lose the XFAIL.", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-29T22:08:31.295252959Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T02:49:47.696010Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "assignee": "cc_1", "closed_at": "2026-04-30T02:49:47.696010Z", "close_reason": "GEOPOS/WITHCOORD coords now use fr-command::format_coord_human (LD_STR_HUMAN — %.17Lf + trailing-zero trim), matching upstream Redis 7.2 byte-for-byte. Pinned by 2 new fr-command regression tests. Shipped in 6af150c."}

 {"id": "frankenredis-yf1j", "title": "Add runtime-populated context fields to Store for INFO accuracy", "description": "# Add runtime context fields to Store for cross-layer INFO reporting

## What
Add fields to Store that the runtime/server populates each tick so that fr-command's INFO handler can report accurate values for fields that originate in the runtime layer:
- pub stat_blocked_clients: u64 (number of currently blocked clients)
- pub stat_tracking_clients: u64 (number of tracking clients)
- pub maxmemory_bytes_live: usize (runtime's current maxmemory setting)

## Why a Store field instead of duplicating logic
The INFO command is dispatched through fr-command's dispatch_argv, which only has access to Store. The runtime has the real values but can't easily pass them through dispatch_argv's signature. The cleanest solution is for the server event loop to update these Store fields before each command batch, so INFO reads accurate values.

## Where to populate
- In fr-server/src/main.rs: before processing each client's commands, update store.stat_blocked_clients = blocked_tokens.len()
- In fr-runtime: when maxmemory changes, update store.maxmemory_bytes_live
- tracking_clients can stay 0 until CLIENT TRACKING is fully wired

## Key implementation points
- Add fields to Store struct with pub visibility
- Initialize to 0 in Store::new()
- Server event loop updates them each tick (cheap — just integer assignments)
- INFO handler in fr-command reads them instead of hardcoding 0

## Why this matters
- blocked_clients:0 is wrong when clients are blocked on BLPOP etc.
- maxmemory:0 in INFO is wrong when runtime has set maxmemory
- Monitoring tools use these for capacity planning

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (Store struct)
- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs (populate fields each tick)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (populate maxmemory_bytes_live on CONFIG SET)", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:18:05.242098041Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-03T03:03:05.788112398Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-03T03:03:05.787855Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

+{"id": "frankenredis-yj7h", "title": "fr-command: MEMORY DOCTOR returns short canned message instead of upstream's verbose empty-instance text", "description": "MEMORY DOCTOR returns 'Sam, I have no memory problems' (one line) for any state. Upstream Redis 7.2 (object.c::getMemoryDoctorReport) has three branches:
  (a) when total_allocated < 5 MB ('empty' branch) → emits the verbose 'Hi Sam, this instance is empty or is using very little memory, my issues detector can't be used in these conditions. Please, leave for your mission on Earth and fill it with some data. The new Sam and I will be back to our programming as soon as I finished rebooting.\n'
  (b) when num_reports == 0 (no issues, not empty) → 'Hi Sam, I can\'t find any memory issue in your instance. I can only account for what occurs on this base.\n'
  (c) when there are issues → a multi-bullet report with sections.

Acceptance: an idle fr-server with no keys returns the (a) verbose empty-instance message verbatim (matching vendored redis-server 7.2.4 byte-for-byte). When dbsize > 0 (or some heuristic for non-empty) but no detected issues, returns the (b) 'I can\'t find any memory issue' line. The (c) verbose-issues path can stay as a follow-up since fr does not yet track allocator/peak/RSS metrics with enough fidelity to surface them.

Reference: ran 'MEMORY DOCTOR' against fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis (port 16701) on a fresh empty instance. fr: 'Sam, I have no memory problems'. redis: 'Hi Sam, this instance is empty or is using very little memory...'. Caught by ad-hoc differential probe.

Fix touches fr-command/src/lib.rs around the MEMORY DOCTOR branch (~line 13487). The conformance fixture core_server.json has 2 cases (memory_doctor + memory_doctor_duplicate_block) pinning the old short message — those need updating.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "assignee": "cc_1", "created_at": "2026-04-30T02:54:06.493297747Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T03:09:20.993170Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "closed_at": "2026-04-30T03:09:20.993170Z", "close_reason": "MEMORY DOCTOR now mirrors upstream object.c::getMemoryDoctorReport empty-instance branch byte-for-byte. Uses dbsize >= 50_000 keys as the empty-vs-non-empty proxy since fr doesn't track allocated bytes. Verified against vendored redis 7.2.4. Conformance fixture core_server.json updated. Shipped in b5a3263."}
